name: Sana Biotechnology, Inc.
business: Sana Biotechnology develops engineered cell therapies using ex vivo and in vivo platforms for type 1 diabetes, oncology, and B cell mediated autoimmune diseases. Its lead candidates are SC451 (HIP-modified stem cell-derived pancreatic islet cells for type 1 diabetes) and SG293 (in vivo CAR T for B cell malignancies and autoimmune diseases). The company has no approved products and has incurred net losses since inception.
sellsTo: B cell mediated autoimmune disease patients

exposures.sentence: We have developed ex vivo and in vivo cell engineering platforms to revolutionize treatment across a broad array of therapeutic areas with unmet treatment needs, including type 1 diabetes, oncology, and B cell mediated autoimmune diseases.

catalysts.sentence: We have incorporated this HIP technology into a more scalable manufacturing platform with SC451 and expect to file an investigational new drug application (IND) as well as begin a Phase 1 clinical trial for this therapy as early as this year.

We have incorporated this HIP technology into a more scalable manufacturing platform with SC451 and expect to file an investigational new drug application (IND) as well as begin a Phase 1 clinical trial for this therapy as early as this year.

catalysts.event: Generate initial clinical data for SG293
catalysts.sentence: We plan to develop SG293 in a range of B cell cancers and B cell mediated autoimmune diseases and expect to generate initial clinical data as early as this year.

catalysts.event: Announced positive 12-month results from UP421 IST
catalysts.sentence: In January 2026, we announced positive 12-month results from the UP421 IST demonstrating that all primary and secondary endpoints were met.

tamClaims.claim: Almost ten million people suffer from type 1 diabetes (T1D) worldwide
tamClaims.sentence: Almost ten million people suffer from type 1 diabetes (T1D) worldwide, and there has been limited progress in treatments for this disease since the advent of insulin injections over 100 years ago.
